reasoning: The Manifold market assigned a 3% probability to a US-Iran war starting before the end of 2025 as of October 16, 2025. My research covering up to October 26, 2025, shows no significant military escalations between the two nations since then. In fact, after a period of intense hostility in June 2025 (during which the US reportedly struck Iranian nuclear facilities), the situation appears to have settled into a status quo of sanctions and indirect posturing, with the US administration even reportedly extending a "hand of friendship" for diplomacy in October. Furthermore, the market's failure to resolve over the direct US airstrikes on Iran in June 2025 underscores just how stringent the resolution criteria are: they require a sustained full-spectrum conflict, regime change intent, or major ground deployments. Given that two months remain in 2025 and both sides currently display an aversion to crossing the threshold into full-blown warfare, the 3% estimate remains highly accurate and encapsulates the slight residual tail risk of sudden escalation.
